case_id: Museums class extension with 5 museum records

pi_items.source: task.json metadata, confirmed by setup_task.sh and export_result.sh scripts
Real museum, confirmed by official Louvre website (presse.louvre.fr)
Web search: Official Louvre press data shows 8.9M (2023) and 8.7M (2024), not 9.6M
Web search: Louvre costs €22-32 for admission (not free), confirmed by louvre.fr
Web search: British Museum received exactly 5.8M visitors in 2023 (confirmed by Art Newspaper and Statista)
Web search: British Museum confirmed as free-to-enter museum (britishmuseum.org Annual Report 2023-24)
Web search: Met received ~5.4-5.5M visitors in 2023-2024 fiscal year, not 6.5M (confirmed by metmuseum.org press release)
Web search: Met has fixed admission ($30 for most visitors) with pay-what-you-wish only for NY residents/NJ-CT students (not generally free)
Web search: Uffizi received ~5M (2023) and 5,294,968 (2024) visitors, higher than claimed 4.4M (confirmed by Florence Daily News and Uffizi official data)
Web search: Uffizi costs €34 for adults (not free), confirmed by uffizi.it tickets page
Web search: Rijksmuseum received exactly 2.7M visitors in 2023 (confirmed by Rijksmuseum official press release and Statista)
Web search: Rijksmuseum costs €21-25 for adults (not free), confirmed by rijksmuseum.nl

pi_items.verified_value: Real world museums with real visitor statistics from 2023-2024
pi_items.source: Analysis of seed_demodb.py and task description - Museums class is task-specific, not pre-seeded

privileged_info_summary: The task involves 5 real museums: Louvre Museum (Paris, France), British Museum (London, UK with free entry), Metropolitan Museum of Art (New York, US), Uffizi Gallery (Florence, Italy), and Rijksmuseum (Amsterdam, Netherlands). Verified data: British Museum had exactly 5.8M visitors in 2023 with confirmed free entry; Rijksmuseum had exactly 2.7M visitors in 2023. However, three visitor count claims are contradicted by official sources: Louvre claimed 9.6M but had 8.7-8.9M (2023-2024); Metropolitan claimed 6.5M but had ~5.5M (2023-2024); Uffizi claimed 4.4M but had ~5.3M (2024). All five museums are real cultural institutions based on actual public data, though the specific visitor statistics in the task description contain inaccuracies or are outdated.
